2604.06518.title: Adaptive Differential Privacy for Federated Medical Image Segmentation Across Diverse Modalities
2604.06518.paper_summary: Large volumes of medical data remain underutilized because centralizing distributed data is often infeasible due to strict privacy regulations and institutional constraints. In addition, models trained in centralized settings frequently fail to generalize across clinical sites because of heterogeneity in imaging protocols and continuously evolving data distributions arising from differences in scanners, acquisition parameters, and patient populations. Federated learning offers a promising solution by enabling collaborative model training without sharing raw data. However, incorporating differential privacy into federated learning, while essential for privacy guarantees, often leads to degraded accuracy, unstable convergence, and reduced generalization. In this work, we propose an adaptive differentially private federated learning (ADP-FL) framework for medical image segmentation that dynamically adjusts privacy mechanisms to better balance the privacy-utility trade-off. The proposed approach stabilizes training, significantly improves Dice scores and segmentation boundary quality, and maintains rigorous privacy guarantees. We evaluated ADP-FL across diverse imaging modalities and segmentation tasks, including skin lesion segmentation in dermoscopic images, kidney tumor segmentation in 3D CT scans, and brain tumor segmentation in multi-parametric MRI. Compared with conventional federated learning and standard differentially private federated learning, ADP-FL consistently achieves higher accuracy, improved boundary delineation, faster convergence, and greater training stability, with performance approaching that of non-private federated learning under the same privacy budgets. These results demonstrate the practical viability of ADP-FL for high-performance, privacy-preserving medical image segmentation in real-world federated settings.
2604.06518.paper_summary_zh: 大量的醫療數據仍未被充分利用，因為集中分散的數據通常因嚴格的隱私法規和機構限制而無法實現。<lb/>此外，在集中環境中訓練的模型常常無法在臨床場所之間進行泛化，這是因為成像協議的異質性以及由於掃描儀、獲取參數和患者群體的差異而不斷演變的數據分佈。<lb/>聯邦學習提供了一個有希望的解決方案，通過不共享原始數據來實現協作模型訓練。<lb/>然而，將差分隱私納入聯邦學習雖然對隱私保障至關重要，但通常會導致準確性下降、不穩定的收斂和降低的泛化能力。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了一個自適應差分隱私聯邦學習（ADP-FL）框架，用於醫療影像分割，該框架動態調整隱私機制，以更好地平衡隱私和效用的權衡。<lb/>所提出的方法穩定了訓練，顯著提高了Dice分數和分割邊界質量，並保持了嚴格的隱私保障。<lb/>我們在多種成像模式和分割任務中評估了ADP-FL，包括在皮膚鏡圖像中的皮膚病變分割、在3D CT掃描中的腎腫瘤分割，以及在多參數MRI中的腦腫瘤分割。<lb/>與傳統的聯邦學習和標準的差分隱私聯邦學習相比，ADP-FL始終實現了更高的準確性、改善的邊界描繪、更快的收斂和更大的訓練穩定性，其性能接近於在相同隱私預算下的非私有聯邦學習。<lb/>這些結果展示了ADP-FL在現實世界聯邦環境中進行高性能、隱私保護的醫療影像分割的實際可行性。
2604.06518.author: Puja Saha et.al.
